figures.caption: Fig. 4: Distribution of optimality ratios for models M1 - M6, capped at 1.01. Only feasible samples are considered.
TABLE I: Various model iterations for each solver. P.P = postprocessing, W.S = warm-start, Th. = threshold, T.R = time ratio, O.R = optimality ratio.

Fig. 3: Predicted generator statuses on a sample data point where the model prediction obtained a lower cost than the ground truth. Grey values indicate the generator was turned off by the post-processing, while light blue indicates the generator was turned on by post-processing.
Fig. 1: The 3 stages of the proposed pipeline. Stage 1 is the deep learning model. Stage 2 is the post-processing heuristics to restore feasibility. Stage 3 is the warm-started MILP solver.

Fig. 2: The components of the proposed architecture. The latent component is used to process the input with positional encoding. The deep encoder consists of self-attention to map the input to the output. Finally, the classification head is responsible for generating the final predictions.
